---
title: "Linear modelling workflow"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source('../assets/setup.R')
```

**You do not need to memorise these steps. This page is here so you can refer to it while working through the analyses for your reports, minidissertations, and dissertations.**

You won't need to use every step below for every analysis, and they don't need to be in this specific order either.
Think of these steps like a buffet to pick and choose from, depending on what your analysis needs.


## Phase 0: Before collecting data

- **Run a power analysis** to see how many participants you'll need to recruit, given the effect size you expect to see or the smallest effect size that's still theoretically interesting.
	- see [DAPR2 power flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#statistical-power-effect-size)


## Phase 1: Before model fitting

- **Load the required R packages and read in your data.**
- **Tidy data** (e.g., any missingness, any implausible values? are data types set correctly?).
- **Identify relevant variables by mapping them to the RQ and data generating process:** What's the outcome? What variables contribute reproducible variability? What grouping variables contribute random variability?
  - see [data generating process flash card](../block1/dgp.html)
  - see [grouping variables flash card](../block1/groupstruc.html)
- **Get summary statistics for the relevant variables.**
  - see [DAPR2 descriptives flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#descriptives)
- **Explore patterns in the data by plotting the relevant variables together.**
  - see [DAPR2 visual exploration flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#visual-exploration)
- **Set up categorical (fixed) predictors (e.g., factor levels? which a priori coding scheme?).**
  - see [DAPR2 categorical predictors flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#numeric-outcomes-categorical-predictors)
- **Set up continuous (fixed) predictors (e.g., any transformations?).**
  - see [DAPR2 data transformation flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#data-transformations)
- **Based on the outcome variable, choose whether to use regular regression or logistic regression.**
  - see [DAPR2 logistic regression flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#logistic-regression)
- **Based on the RQ, decide whether the RQ requires an interaction model.**
  - see [DAPR2 interaction flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#interaction-models)
- **Identify maximal model.**
  - see [maximal model flash card](../block1/maxmodel.html)


## Phase 2: Model fitting and troubleshooting

- **Try fitting the maximal model to the data.**
- **If convergence errors: Try a different optimiser and fit model again.**
  - see [model troubleshooting flash card](../block1/troubleshoot.html)
- **If continued convergence errors or singular fit: Simplify random effect structure and fit model again.**
  - see [model simplification flash card](../block1/simplify.html)
- **If suggestion to rescale: Convert continuous predictor(s) to z-scores and fit model again.**
  - see [DAPR2 standardisation flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#data-transformations)


## Phase 3: After model fitting

- **Check model assumptions.**
   - see [DAPR2 regular regression assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#linear-models-2)
   - see [DAPR2 logistic regression assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#logistic-models-3)
   - see [LMM assumptions flash card](../block1/assump.html)
  - **If model relies on assumptions about normality/equal variance of errors and if these are violated, bootstrap model estimates.**
	  - see [DAPR2 bootstrapping flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#bootstrap)
- **Run diagnostics for multicollinearity.**
  - see [DAPR2 assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#linear-models-2)
- **Run diagnostics for outlying and influential data points (aka case diagnostics).**
   - see [DAPR2 regular regression assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#linear-models-2)
   - see [DAPR2 logistic regression assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#logistic-models-3)
   - **If outlying/influential data points detected: run sensitivity analysis.**
      - see [DAPR2 regular regression assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#linear-models-2)
      - see [DAPR2 logistic regression assumptions flash card](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#logistic-models-3)
- **Interpret the model estimates.**
  - see [DAPR2 statistical models flash cards](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#statistical-models)
  - see [DAPR2 logistic regression flash cards](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#logistic-regression) 
  - see [DAPR2 interaction models flash cards](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#interaction-models)
  - see [interpreting random effects flash card](../block1/interp.html)
  - see [p-values for LMMs flash card](../block1/pvals.html)
- **If model contains interactions:**
  - Compute simple slopes/simple effects (incl. corrections for multiple comparisons, if necessary).
    - see [DAPR2 num/cat interaction flash cards](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#numeric-x-categorical-example)
  - Plot simple slopes/simple effects.
    - see [DAPR2 num/cat interaction flash cards](https://uoepsy.github.io/dapr2/2526/labs/1_b4_reading.html#numeric-x-categorical-example)
- **Plot fixed effects along with group-level variability.**
  - see [plotting random effects flash card](../block1/plot-ranefs.qmd)
  - see [plotting fitted values](../block1/plot-fitted.qmd)
- **Write up your analysis and results.**
  - see [reporting LMMs flash card](../block1/reporting.html)
